lede: A look at August 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Neil Armstrong and Guy de Maupassant, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Biman Bangladesh Airlines
events.desc: A Biman Bangladesh Airlines Fokker F27 Friendship crashes on approach to Zia International Airport, in Dhaka, Bangladesh, killing all 49 people on board.

events.title: Ronald Reagan
events.desc: President Ronald Reagan fires 11,359 striking air-traffic controllers who ignored his order for them to return to work.

events.title: Marilyn Monroe
events.desc: American actress Marilyn Monroe is found dead at her home from a drug overdose.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The German minelayer SS Königin Luise lays a minefield about 40 miles (64 km) off the Thames Estuary (Lowestoft). She is intercepted and sunk by the British light-cruiser HMS Amphion.

events.title: Bertha Benz
events.desc: Bertha Benz drives from Mannheim to Pforzheim and back in the first long distance automobile trip, commemorated as the Bertha Benz Memorial Route since 2008.

events.title: Beaver Wars
events.desc: Beaver Wars: Fifteen hundred Iroquois attack Lachine in New France.

events.title: John Ruthven, 3rd Earl of Gowrie
events.desc: The Gowrie Conspiracy against King James VI of Scotland (later to become King James I of England) takes place at Gowrie House (Perth, Scotland).

events.title: Capture of Roxburgh (1460)
events.desc: The Kingdom of Scotland captures Roxburgh, one of the last English strongholds in Scotland, following a siege.

events.title: Henry I of England
events.desc: Henry I is crowned King of England in Westminster Abbey.

events.title: AD 25
events.desc: Guangwu claims the throne as Emperor of China, restoring the Han dynasty after the collapse of the short-lived Xin dynasty.

births.name: Neil Armstrong
births.line: American pilot, engineer, and astronaut, first man to walk on the Moon

births.name: Guy de Maupassant
births.line: French short story writer, novelist, and poet

births.name: Jesse Williams (actor)
births.line: American actor, director, producer, and political activist

births.name: Wassily Leontief
births.line: German-American economist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Toni Morrison, American author, Pulitzer Prize winner, and Nobel laureate .
Todor Zhivkov, Bulgarian commander and politician, 36th Prime Minister of Bulgaria
Otto Kretschmer, German naval officer and submariner
John Ruthven, 3rd Earl of Gowrie, Scottish conspirator

observances.text: Christian feast day: Dedication of the Basilica of St Mary Major (Catholic Church)
